definitions.text: To heap up in a mass; to pile up; to collect or bring together (either literally or figuratively), often gradually and without active intent.
examples: He wishes to accumulate a sum of money.

definitions.text: To gradually grow or increase in quantity or number.
examples: With her company going bankrupt, her divorce, and a gambling habit, debts started to accumulate so she had to sell her house.

definitions.text: To take a higher degree at the same time with a lower degree, or at a shorter interval than usual.
